evidence: We have invested, and expect to continue to invest, significant resources to build and support our AI products.

Fiscal 2026 Plan intended to drive customer satisfaction, product innovation, and productivity through artificial intelligence adoption and enablement... HP expects to reduce global headcount by approximately 4,000 to 6,000 employees... pre-tax charges of approximately $650 million.
The Fiscal 2026 Plan is expected to run for three years through the end of fiscal year 2028. We expect to invest some of the savings into our growth areas and our people.
Our high-performing product portfolio includes HP's new line of AI PCs and workstations built with the computing power to enable local AI processing for enhanced performance and features.
Our platforms incorporate processors from Intel, and AMD, including integrated AI acceleration, as well as NVIDIA GPUs for advanced graphics and compute workloads.
Since announcing our Fiscal 2023 Plan, we have enhanced our digital capabilities in Workforce Solutions and continued to leverage AI to positively impact our products, solutions and operations.

AI algorithms may be flawed. Datasets may be insufficient or contain biased information. AI models deployed by us or our partners may lead to unexpected or unintended outcomes that could erode trust in our AI products.
Our actions to drive demand to AI products may result in cannibalization of demand for our traditional, non-AI products, which may result in lower net revenues and/or profit margins.

Proliferating export controls, including controls on certain rare earth elements, designed to limit the diffusion of advanced technology, can impact speed on AI innovation and capabilities by controlling hardware capable of supporting advanced AI technology.

capex_mentions.quote: HP estimates that it will incur pre-tax charges of approximately $650 million relating to labor and non-labor actions
capex_mentions.context: Fiscal 2026 Plan restructuring charges tied to AI adoption and enablement, running through fiscal 2028

limitations: The $650 million figure is restructuring/charge estimate for the Fiscal 2026 Plan broadly (AI adoption and enablement plus workforce reductions), not a pure AI R&D or capex figure; AI-attributable portion is not separately quantified.

No dedicated AI R&D dollar amounts or AI-specific capex figures are disclosed in the excerpts; investment intensity score relies on the restructuring plan cost and qualitative statements about ongoing AI investment.

Narrative concreteness is scored 4 (deployed products with operating detail) rather than 5 because no quantified business results (revenue lift, units sold, efficiency metrics) from AI PC deployments appear in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure excerpts are extensive and include risk-factor-section material; score reflects company-specific risk mechanisms described but no quantified risk exposure or named governance oversight body for AI specifically.
